lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the East Side is still climbing. I've got the numbers right here — Tucker sent them over during the break — and it's tracking almost exactly like last year at this point. Which, for the record, was when I was still pretending to have a legitimate job.

If you look at the hourly delta pattern from midnight to now, what you're seeing is a fatigue curve, but with secondary activity kicking in. The organized groups are settling. The opportunistic are waking up.
And the wind's shifted northwest — gusting to eighteen now. Visibility's down to maybe three miles. That affects sightlines. That affects behavior.

Caroline's right. And you know who knew that was coming? Director Calloway. Because he had the weather briefing before we did. Because he's in a room with reinforced glass and we're broadcasting from a shipping container on East Washington.

The irony is — and I've calculated this — his security detail costs more per hour than most neighborhood watch operations spend in an entire Purge year. So statistically, he's safer tonight than any barricaded family on Atwood is.

That's not irony. That's design. Holden, the forecast is actually what matters. We're expecting the wind to ease around 04:00. That could suppress activity or concentrate it — we won't know until it happens.
See, this is why I drink and she doesn't. She's got data. I've got rage and a recording contract that expires at 07:00 AM. Stay with us.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 104 ({{ELAPSED}} into Purge). East Side: 31. Capitol Square holding at 28.
Wind advisory: NW 18 mph, gusts to 24. Visibility 2.5 miles. Fog advisory Lake Mendota shoreline.
Willy Street barricade remains intact. Zero breaches reported. Neighborhood watch commander standing by.
Text from James, Park Street: 'Are the power outages planned or should I assume worst?' — Tim's response: check your breaker first.
